question_text: Match the items in Column I with their descriptions in Column II:

column_b.text: Issued by banks to raise short-term funds from depositors
Issued by RBI on behalf of the government as a zero coupon bond
Overnight or short-term interbank lending in the money market
Unsecured promissory note issued by large corporations for short-term financing

explanation: Treasury Bill is issued by RBI on behalf of the government as a zero coupon bond. Commercial Paper is an unsecured promissory note issued by large corporations. Certificate of Deposit is issued by banks to raise short-term funds. Call Money refers to overnight or short-term interbank lending.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Treasury Bill is not issued by banks; it is issued by RBI on behalf of the government.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing Certificate of Deposit (issued by banks) with Treasury Bill.

elimination_hints.hint: Commercial Paper is not issued by banks for depositors; it is issued by corporations.
elimination_hints.misconception: Mixing up the issuer of Commercial Paper with Certificate of Deposit.

elimination_hints.hint: Call Money does not refer to a zero coupon bond; it refers to short-term interbank lending.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing Call Money with Treasury Bill characteristics.

question_text: Match the items in Column I with their descriptions in Column II:

column_b.text: A public offering by a company that is already listed on the stock exchange
Market where previously issued securities are bought and sold
The first time a company offers its shares to the general public
Market where new securities are issued for the first time

explanation: Primary Market is where new securities are issued for the first time. Secondary Market is where previously issued securities are traded. IPO (Initial Public Offering) is when a company first offers shares to the public. FPO (Follow-on Public Offering) is a public offering by an already listed company.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Primary Market deals with new securities, not the first public offering by a company.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing Primary Market with IPO; Primary Market is the market, IPO is a type of issue.

elimination_hints.hint: Primary Market is not where existing securities are traded; that describes the Secondary Market.
elimination_hints.misconception: Swapping definitions of Primary and Secondary Markets.

elimination_hints.hint: FPO is not where new securities are first issued; it is a follow-on offering by an already listed company.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing FPO with Primary Market function.

question_text: Match the items in Column I with their descriptions in Column II:

column_b.text: Regulatory function of SEBI
Developmental function of SEBI
Protective function of SEBI
Developmental function of SEBI

explanation: Prohibiting insider trading is a Protective function as it protects investors. Training intermediaries is a Developmental function aimed at developing the market. Registration of brokers is a Regulatory function as it involves regulation. Promoting fair practices is a Developmental function aimed at market development.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Prohibiting insider trading is not a Regulatory function; it is specifically aimed at protecting investors.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing Protective and Regulatory functions of SEBI.

elimination_hints.hint: Prohibiting insider trading is not a Developmental function; it directly safeguards investor interests.
elimination_hints.misconception: Misclassifying investor protection measures as developmental activities.

elimination_hints.hint: Training intermediaries is not a Regulatory function; it is aimed at developing market infrastructure.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing Developmental activities with Regulatory oversight.

question_text: Match the items in Column I with their descriptions in Column II:

column_b.text: The broker matches the buy and sell orders on the stock exchange
Transfer of securities and funds between buyer and seller
Investor instructs the broker to buy or sell securities
An account to hold securities in electronic form with a depository participant

explanation: Opening Demat Account involves creating an account with a depository participant to hold securities electronically. Placing Order is when the investor instructs the broker. Executing Order is when buy and sell orders are matched. Settlement is the final transfer of securities and funds.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Opening Demat Account is not about matching buy and sell orders; it is the first step of opening an electronic account.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing the account opening step with the order execution step.

elimination_hints.hint: Opening Demat Account does not involve instructing a broker; that is the Placing Order step.
elimination_hints.misconception: Mixing up account setup with placing a trade order.

elimination_hints.hint: Settlement is not about instructing the broker; it is the final transfer of securities and funds.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing the settlement process with the order placement process.

question_text: Match the items in Column I with their descriptions in Column II:

column_b.text: Guarantees the sale of securities by agreeing to buy unsold shares
Statutory regulatory body established to protect investor interests
An institution that holds securities in electronic form on behalf of investors
A registered intermediary who buys and sells securities on behalf of clients

explanation: A Broker is a registered intermediary who trades securities on behalf of clients. An Underwriter guarantees the sale of securities by purchasing any unsold shares. A Depository holds securities electronically on behalf of investors. SEBI is the statutory regulatory body that protects investor interests in the securities market.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: A Broker does not guarantee purchase of unsold shares; that is the role of an Underwriter.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing the intermediary role of a Broker with the guarantee function of an Underwriter.

elimination_hints.hint: A Broker does not hold securities electronically; that is the function of a Depository.
elimination_hints.misconception: Mixing up the trading role of a Broker with the custodial role of a Depository.

elimination_hints.hint: SEBI is not an intermediary that trades on behalf of clients; it is the regulatory authority.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing SEBI's regulatory role with that of a market intermediary.
